artifact_title: Scheduler Migration Note: Evidence Sufficiency for Artifact Non-Inferiority Experiments

claims.text: SchedulerClock is established as the deterministic semantic baseline for scheduler experiments.
No production scheduler components (daemon, sleep loop, wall-clock SLA, process supervisor) were built.
Paired DES and wall-clock smoke tests passed for due ordering, expiration, next-wake reporting, and restart frontier recovery.

conclusion: The scheduler evidence (deterministic semantic baseline, wall-clock smoke test pass, and absence of a production scheduler) is sufficient to proceed with artifact non-inferiority experiments.
declared_losses: Production scheduler was not built, so production non-inferiority cannot be directly assessed; this note only covers artifact non-inferiority.
